titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Chris Ogden Papers
titlestmt.author: Elizabeth Nosari

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday April 10, 2026 at 03:00

descriptivenote.p: Chiefly in English. Small amount of material in Russian, German, French, and Danish.

did.abstract: This collection documents the life and career of Christopher "Chris" Ogden, particularly during his tenure as a journalist and photojournalist at Time magazine and the publication of his major biographical works. It includes personal papers and memorabilia such as curricula vitae, press clippings, correspondence, notebooks, calendars, and press passes. The collection also contains files such as interviews, correspondence, and reviews related to Ogden's biographies Maggie: An Intimate Portrait of a Woman in Power (1990), Life of the Party: The Biography of Pamela Digby Churchill Hayward Harriman (1994), and Legacy: A Biography of Moses and Walter Annenberg (1999). Additional materials related to his journalism career include articles, lectures, photographs, and portfolios, including prints of his portrait of Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn (1974). Also present are computer files, including scanned or digitized photographs and portfolios as well as digitized personal papers and materials related to publications.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Christopher Ogden Papers (MS 2128). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Christopher "Chris" Bennett Ogden (1945–2022) was born February 9, 1945, in Providence, Rhode Island, to Agnes "Agga" Bennett Ogden and Michael Joseph Ogden, a journalist and managing editor of Providence Journal . Family trips, including a year-long sabbatical through post-war Europe in 1952, enabled Ogden to develop a lifelong interest in travel at a young age. He graduated from Portsmouth Abbey School in 1962 and Yale University in 1966 with a bachelor of arts in history. After completing university, he served as an Army intelligence officer and interpreter during the Vietnam War, 1966–1970.

Following his military service, Ogden began a career in journalism at United Press International (UPI) in London, England, in 1970, before relocating with UPI to Moscow, Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (USSR), in 1972. In 1974, he was hired by Time magazine as a foreign correspondent in Moscow, before relocating to a correspondent role in Los Angeles, California, in 1975. Ogden worked for Time for almost thirty years, including as a senior diplomatic correspondent with the State Department and White House correspondent in Washington, D.C., 1976–1981. He went on to serve as Midwest bureau chief in Chicago, Illinois, 1981–1985, and bureau chief in London, England, 1985–1989, before returning to Washington, D.C., 1989–1991, where he wrote his column, "A View from Washington." In 1990, Ogden published Maggie , a biography of Margaret Thatcher. The following year, in 1991, he left Time and focused on writing the biographies Life of the Party: The Biography of Pamela Digby Churchill Hayward Harriman , 1994, and Legacy: A Biography of Moses and Walter Annenberg , 1999.

Ogden was also a photojournalist who published photographs under the pseudonym "Chris Bennett." His portraits of author and Soviet dissident Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn (1918–2008), for example, accompanied his interview published in Time magazine on February 25, 1974, and also achieved global syndication. The close-up portrait of Solzhenitsyn featured as the cover art for that issue—likely Ogden's most widely known photograph—is held in the collection of the National Portrait Gallery, Washington, D.C.

Ogden married artist Diana May "Deedy" Ogden in 1967, and together they had two children, Michael Ogden and Margaret Ogden; they divorced in 2000. He was married to Linda Marlene Fuselier from 2010 until his death on August 27, 2022, in Honolulu, Hawaii.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Most folder titles have been transcribed directly from Christopher Ogden's original folders. Devised folder titles and additions to existing folder titles have been enclosed in brackets.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 11 and 12 (audiocassette tapes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 13 (floppy disk): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

Series IV and Series V (computer files): Restricted fragile material. Original computer files may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies instead of the originals. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright and other associated proprietary rights for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Christopher Ogden was transferred to Yale University in 2022. These materials may be used for non-commercial purposes without seeking permission from Yale University as the copyright holder. For other uses of these materials, please contact beinecke.library@yale.edu.

Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized in five series: I. Personal papers, 1953–2022, undated. II. Writings, 1963–2021. III. Photographs and portfolios, 1960–circa 1990s, undated. IV. Photograph and portfolio computer files, 1967–2022, undated. V. Laptop files, 1911–2022, undated

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection documents the life and career of Christopher "Chris" Ogden. Personal papers, 1953–2022, include personal files and memorabilia such as correspondence, a résumé, a curriculum vitae, notebooks from early travels, press passes, calendars, and collected publications with inscriptions from Stanley Karnow and Richard Nixon, among others. Writings, 1963–2021, consist of interviews, correspondence, reviews, and press clippings related to biographies Maggie: An Intimate Portrait of a Woman in Power (1990), Life of the Party: The Biography of Pamela Digby Churchill Hayward Harriman (1994), and Legacy: A Biography of Moses and Walter Annenberg (1999). Additional writing materials include articles, 1963–2000, and lectures, 1986–circa 2013. Photographs and portfolios, 1960–circa 1990s, are comprised of portraits related to Ogden's photojournalism career, including of Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn, Zbigniew Brzezinski, and Andrei Sakharov. This series also contains personal and travel photographs as well as portfolios "Banquet: The Global Photos," "Portraits: 75@75," "Street Art: Europe, India, Israel, the Americas," and "Street Art: Rome." Photograph and portfolio computer files, circa 2022, are comprised of digitized photographs and photograph portfolios. Laptop computer files, circa 2022, are comprised of Ogden's computer files, including scans of personal and professional documents as well as scanned and born-digital photographs.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The original file structure for the born digital computer files in this collection has been maintained.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes Ogden's personal files, which are comprised of biographical and travel timelines, a résumé, a curriculum vitae, clippings related to his career, articles, photocopies of Army records, calendars, and notebooks from early travels to Louisville, New Orleans, Texas, California, and Mexico. Personal files also contain some correspondence, including from Jimmy Carter and Richard Nixon. Memorabilia consists of Ogden's business cards, press passes, membership cards such as for the Time Life Alumni Society, and his Boy Scouts of America Life Scouts certificate. Also present are publications from Ogen's personal library with inscriptions from authors, including Richard "Dick" Valeriani, Zbigniew Brzezinski, Stanley Karnow, and Richard Nixon.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized in two subseries and chronologically therein: Personal files and memorabilia, 1953–2022, undated. Collected publications, 1972–1997.

did.unittitle: [Notebook] - Log of Western Trip - California and misc.
[Notebook] - Summer - New Orleans, Texas Bracketville, Mexico
[Alexander Solzehenitsyn. August 1914 (New York: Farrar, Straus, and Giroux, 1972).]
[Richard Valeriani. Travels with Henry (Boston: Houghton Mifflin, 1979).]
[Zbigniew Brzezinski. Power and Principle: Memoirs of the National Security Adviser, 1977-1981 (New York: Farrar, Straus, and Giroux, 1983).]
[Stanley Karnow. In Our Image: America's Empire in the Phillippines (New York: Ballantine Books, 1989).]
[Winston S. Churchill. Thoughts and Adventures (London: Mandarin, 1990).]
[Richard Nixon. In the Arena: A Memoir of Victory, Defeat, and Renewal (New York: Simon and Schuster, 1990).]
[Stanley Karnow. Paris in the Fifties (New York: Times Books, 1997).]

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 11 and 12 (audiocassette tapes): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information. Box 13 (floppy disk): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials related to Maggie: An Intimate Portrait of a Woman in Power (1990) consist of correspondence and clippings of reviews and book press coverage as well as Ogden's obituary of Margaret Thatcher. Also present is a copy of Maggie as well as a copy of the Russian-language version of the publication, published in 1992.

Materials related to Life of the Party: The Biography of Pamela Digby Churchill Hayward Harriman (1994), include audiocassette tape recordings and transcriptions of research interviews, 1991–1993, with Pamela Churchill Harriman, William "Bill" Walton, Ben Bradlee, Bill Hayward, Brooke Hayward, Peter Duchin, Edward Digby (12th Baron Digby), Piers Dixon, Louise Grunwald, and Panagiotis "Taki" Theodoracopulos. Materials also include correspondence and written questions with Janet Murrow, a copy of the publication, correspondence, and clippings of book reviews and book press coverage.

Materials related to Legacy: A Biography of Moses and Walter Annenberg (1999), include correspondence, advertising copy, clippings of book reviews and press, notes, photographs, and a list of sources. Also present are transcripts of interviews with Wallis Annenberg, 1997, and Marie Ridder, 1995. The floppy disk "WHA - Office Mac - Backup Files" includes an unrelated fax from Ogden to James Hanson (Baron Hanson).

This series also contains materials related to additional, privately commissioned biography projects. Materials related to Ogden's unwritten book about Sanford "Sandy" I. Weill (b. 1933) include Ogden's narrative account of the project, the project contract, and Weill's responses to questions, 2001. Materials related to "Aaron's Gift," a book about early Sears Roebuck and Company investor and owner Aaron E. Nusbaum (later Aaron E. Norman; 1859–1936), include a timeline of family financial management and correspondence, 2001–2011.

Articles are primarily comprised of clippings as well as some typescripts of articles written by Ogden during his tenure at United Press International (UPI), 1970–1972, and Time , 1972–2000. Articles also include some notes and Time covers featuring Ogden's articles and photographs as well as writing portfolios containing cover art and clippings: "Deadline UK: The Wire Stories, 1970–1972," "Deadline USSR: The Wire Stories, 1972–1974," and "Washington DC Time Magazine, 1991–1996."
Lectures include lecture typescripts and notes as well as the bound volume "Passport: The Global Lectures."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized in three subseries and chronologically therein: Biographies, 1985–2021. Articles, 1963–2000. Lectures, 1986–circa 2013.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Please note that not all audiocassette tape recordings of interviews from Ogden's Life of the Party research have equivalent transcriptions and not all transcriptions have equivalent audiocassette tape recordings.

scopecontent.p: Folder includes transcripts of interviews that took place on the following dates: July 1, 1991; July 7, 1991; August 1, 1991; August 2, 1991; and August 3, 1991.

title.part: Legacy: A Biography of Moses and Walter Annenberg

did.unittitle: [Floppy disk] WHA - Office Mac - Backup Files - Feb 1996-June 30, 96
did.physdesc: 1.47 Megabytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: [Publisher Letter June 2, 1975, "Alaska: Rush for Riches on the Great Pipeline"]
Lectures not in Passport or Campus

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographic prints as well as some photographic negatives, magazine clippings, and slides. Photographs include snapshot portraits of Ogden's parents, Agnes Bennett Ogden and Michael Ogden as well as Ogden's first wife, Diana May "Deedy" Ogden. Also included are snapshots of Ogden traveling in places such as Taiwan and Russia as well as portraits of Ogden speaking or posing with Margaret Thatcher, Walter Annenberg, Fidel Castro, and Jimmy Carter, among others.

This series also contains Ogden's portraits of Andrei Sakharov (1974), Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn (1974), Zbigniew Brzeziński (1976), and Mikhail Gorbachev (1993) in various formats, including prints and news clippings. The folders related to Ogden's portraits of Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn contain additional material, including Ogden's recollection of his experience with Solzhenitsyn and his Time World report.

Additionally, this series is comprised of portfolios created by Ogden, circa 2022, containing professional and travel-related photographic work from of the course of his life and career, including "Banquet: The Global Photos," "Portraits: 75@75," "Street Art, Europe, India, Israel the Americas," and "Street Art, Rome."

did.unittitle: Solzhenitsyn [portrait clippings in German, French, and Danish]
"Street Art: Europe, India, Israel the Americas"
Series IV: Photograph and portfolio computer files

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Ogden's born-digital curricula vitae files have been printed and organized in Series 1 subseries Personal files and memorabilia, 1953–2022, undated, in the folder [Curriculum vitae], circa 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized in Ogden's original file structure. Includes the following folders and computer file.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series is comprised of computer files created circa 2022, including digitized or scanned photographs. It primarily consists of cover art and photograph files related to Ogden's photograph portfolios, which feature selected professional and travel-related images by Ogden spanning the course of his life and career. Included in this series are "USSR: Before the Fall," "Nomad: A Traveler's Collection," and "Silk Road." Also included are "Street Art: Rome," "Street Art: Europe, India, Israel, the Americas," and "Banquet: The Global Photos," which can also be found in print form in Series III. This series also consists of digitized images arranged in folders, including "Yale (Leaders)," "Ogden's Portraits," and "Yale (SakSolzh)," some of which are included in print form in Series III. The folder, "Yale Covers," contains photograph and writing portfolio cover art created by Ogden, many of which can be found in print form in Series II and Series III.
Outliers in this series include a PDF file of "Passport: The Global Lectures" (found in Series II) and the folder "CV_Ogden" containing curricula vitae (found in Series I).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Original computer files may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies instead of the originals. Consult Access Services for further information.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: The computer files in this series (accession 2022.m.0025) were transferred by Christopher Ogden to Yale University in 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized in Ogden's original file structure. In addition to assorted unfoldered computer files, arranged in the following folders.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Original computer files may not be accessed due to their fragility. Researchers must consult access copies instead of the originals. Consult Access Services for further information.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Christopher Ogden's laptop computer files (accession 2024.m.0008) were transferred by Linda Fuselier to Yale University in 2023.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Ogen's laptop computer files and includes a wide range of materials related to the author's life and career. Many of these files are scans or digitized duplicates of photographs and records found in Series I–IV of this collection, created circa 2022.

The folders "CBO" [Chris Bennet Ogden] and "Veterans" include Army records. "Writing" and "Time Mag" folders contain computer files and scans related to writing for Time magazine, scans of press passes, a scan of 1991 book Desert Storm: The War in the Persian Gulf by the Editors of Time Magazine (Otto Friedrich, ed.), and scans of press coverage and published works. "Books - publishers" contains correspondence and materials related to Ogden's biographical book projects. "Yale" contains scans of materials transferred to Yale and included in Series I–III, including personal papers and correspondence and materials related to his portfolios or scrapbooks of photography and writing.

Travel and family photographs, including of his wife Linda Fuselier and children, as well as other files like maps can be found in folders "1a Sing - Perth Oct 2022," "Asia Notes," "Photos," and "Photos Library.photoslibrary."
